_comment: Job application + screening: one round-trip. Remove _comment keys before strict JSON parsing.

flow.step1_browserToNext: Candidate POSTs multipart to Next.js POST /api/job-applications (field cv = file, plus jobSlug, firstName, lastName, email, phone).
flow.step2_asyncDefault: By default (JOB_APPLICATION_WEBHOOK_SYNC unset/false), Next responds immediately with { ok, id, screeningStatus: "pending" } and runs the Java webhook in the background (next/server after). Next stores backendPersonId on the Firestore doc only — not screening. webhookCompletedAt marks completion.
flow.step3_portal: Recruiters see screening in the portal when GET BACKEND_TENANT_APPLICATIONS_* returns screening keyed by Firestore applicationId (merge). The public apply form does not show screening.
flow.step4_syncOption: Set JOB_APPLICATION_WEBHOOK_SYNC=true to wait for the webhook before responding; then the first response may include screening and backendPersonId inline (screeningStatus: "complete") — same JSON shape as browserResponseExample.
flow.step5_backendWebhook: Next POSTs multipart to BACKEND_JOB_APPLICATION_* URL: field file = CV, plus applicationId, tenantId, jobRef, jobSlug, jobTitle, companyName, vacancyId, firstName, lastName, email, phone, cvStoragePath, cvFileName, cvContentType. Header X-Tenant-Id.
flow.step6_backendResponse: Webhook JSON (JobApplicationResponse): id, optional screening — only applicant id is written to Firestore. For portal screening, your GET /api/tenant/{tenantId}/applications (or BACKEND_TENANT_APPLICATIONS_URL) should return applications[] with applicationId + screening per row. Do not include candidateName in screening if you keep PII out of screening payloads — the UI uses job context only.
flow.step7_display: Recruiter: ApplicationsPanel + CandidateScreeningCard (screening merged from tenant GET). Public apply: thank-you only.

match.headline: Strong alignment on backend ownership and scale
match.summary: The candidate shows eight years of backend experience with clear ownership of high-traffic services. The stack overlaps strongly with this role (TypeScript, Postgres, Kubernetes). Leadership experience is lighter than the ideal profile but coaching and code quality practices are evident.
insights: Seniority and years of experience match the job level.
Domain overlap with B2B SaaS and billing systems is a plus.
Limited evidence of cross-team technical leadership at director level — flag for interview focus.
match.rationale: Recommend phone screen: technical fit is high; validate people leadership expectations in first conversation.

attributes.experience: 8+ years backend; 2 years as tech lead

asyncFirstResponseExample._note: Default (async webhook): candidate gets this immediately after apply — no screening yet.

_notes: screening is produced by your backend after it receives the CV (multipart) and runs your screening engine.
If screening is omitted or invalid, apply still succeeds; the UI falls back to the generic thank-you message.
Return screening next to id at the webhook root, or nested under data.screening — see parseCandidateScreeningFromBackendPayload in lib/candidate-screening-result.ts.
